title: Evidence-Based Falls Risk Screening for Clinicians

seo.title: Falls Risk Screening for Clinicians | Able Care
seo.description: Multifactorial, peer-reviewed falls risk screening. Standardized capture for PTs, OTs, geriatricians and research teams. Built for clinical workflows.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Clinician guiding patient through GripAble assessment
blocks.headline: Multifactorial, evidence-based falls risk screening.
blocks.subtitle: Peer-reviewed protocols. Standardized capture. Built for clinical and research teams.

blocks.heading: Clinical-grade data, captured digitally
blocks.body: Able Assess replaces subjective paper scoring with four peer-reviewed measurements captured by the GripAble sensor. Grip strength, sit-to-stand, gait speed and timed up-and-go in one standardized workflow. Results flow directly into the clinical record.
blocks.imageAlt: Clinician reviewing Able Assess results on a tablet with a patient

blocks.heading: How it works in your clinical workflow

steps.title: Capture
steps.description: Run a five-minute multifactorial assessment using the GripAble sensor and any tablet.

steps.title: Score
steps.description: Instant risk banding against normative data. Four validated metrics scored automatically.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.description: Results route into EHR, OASIS or your existing clinical system.

steps.title: Track
steps.description: Longitudinal trends surface functional decline early, supporting evidence-based care planning.

blocks.heading: Four metrics, one picture of function

metrics.name: Grip strength
metrics.description: Upper-body strength proxy. Predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure (Lancet, 2015).

metrics.name: Sit-to-stand
metrics.description: Lower-limb power and balance. Validated marker of functional capacity and fall risk.

metrics.name: Gait speed
metrics.description: The 'sixth vital sign' for older adults. Linked to mortality, hospitalization and functional decline.

metrics.name: Timed Up and Go
metrics.description: Falls risk in under a minute. One of the most widely validated functional assessments in geriatric medicine.

blocks.heading: Backed by the research
blocks.pulledStat: Grip strength predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure.

citations.title: Prognostic value of grip strength: findings from the Prospective Urban Rural Epidemiology (PURE) study
Multifactorial falls risk assessment outperforms single-test approaches in community-dwelling older adults

testimonials.quote: We caught decline six weeks earlier than we would have with our old process.
testimonials.attribution: Clinical Director, US home care agency

testimonials.quote: The data quality is a step change from paper. We can finally track functional trends over time.

items.question: How does Able Assess compare to paper-based assessments like Morse or Tinetti?
items.answer: Paper assessments are scored subjectively, take 20+ minutes and rarely produce a data trail. Able Assess captures four objective metrics in under five minutes, scores automatically against normative data, and integrates with your clinical record.

items.question: What evidence supports the four metrics?
items.answer: Each metric is backed by peer-reviewed research. Grip strength alone is supported by the PURE study (Lancet, 2015), which found it predicts all-cause mortality more reliably than systolic blood pressure. The full evidence base is available in our research library.

items.question: Does it integrate with EHR systems?
items.answer: Yes. Able Assess is integration-ready with EHR, OASIS and clinical platforms. The clinical team can walk you through the setup during a demo.

items.question: Can non-clinical staff run the assessment?
items.answer: Yes. The workflow was designed so that any member of staff can run it with minimal training. This frees clinicians to focus on interpretation and care planning.

items.question: Is the GripAble sensor clinically validated?
items.answer: Yes. The sensor is FDA listed, CE marked, and commercialized out of Imperial College London neuroscience research.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your clinical workflow.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has run it in the field.
